parts.found: Spotted! A prize for doing nothing is almost always a trick. 🚩
The big one! Nobody real ever needs your password to give you a prize. 🚩

parts.hint: The hurry is part of the trick, but the real danger is what it asks you to type.

spot.prompt: A window just popped up over your video. TWO things about it are fishy, can you tap them both?
spot.missHint: Close. Look at what it promises, and what it asks you to type.

decide.prompt: So what do we do about this pop-up?

choices.feedback: That pop-up is a trick called phishing. Real games never ask for your password to give you a prize.
Exactly right. If a prize sounds too good to be true, it almost always is. Closing the window costs you nothing.
Kind thought! But passing on a trick spreads it. Better to close it and warn your friend.

messages.parentTag: pop-ups and prizes that ask for passwords

react.takeaway: Nobody legitimate will ever ask for your password, not a game, not a website, not a stranger.
